news.title:
MarketSmith India recommends Voltamp Transformers and BHEL for 13 April, citing strong fundamentals and technical breakouts.
Sensex surged 900+ points; Nifty 50 hit 24,050. Banking heavyweights like ICICI, HDFC, and Axis Bank led gains.
news.summary:
MarketSmith India recommends Voltamp Transformers and BHEL for 13 April, citing strong fundamentals and technical breakouts.

news.title:
Mangalam Worldwide gains vendor approvals from QatarEnergy, BHEL, EIL, and GSFC.
Stock closed at ₹261.80, down 1.45%; YTD decline at 6.3%; 1-year gain at 64%.

news.title:
BHEL surged 15.85% from Rs.245.70 to Rs.284.65, outperforming Sensex's 5.34% gain.
The rally was driven by positive technical shifts, robust volumes, and institutional interest.
news.summary:
BHEL surged 15.85% from Rs.245.70 to Rs.284.65, outperforming Sensex's 5.34% gain.
The rally was driven by positive technical shifts, robust volumes, and institutional interest.

news.title:
India's nuclear push creates long-term opportunities for engineering firms like L&T, BHEL, focusing on indigenization.
L&T faces near-term challenges due to Middle East tensions but may benefit from rebuilding demand medium-term; valuations remain a concern.
news.summary:
India's nuclear push creates long-term opportunities for engineering firms like L&T, BHEL, focusing on indigenization.
L&T faces near-term challenges due to Middle East tensions but may benefit from rebuilding demand medium-term; valuations remain a concern.

news.title:
BHEL partnered with E2S Korea for advanced excitation systems tech.
Deal enables design, manufacture, and servicing in India, boosting Make in India.
news.summary:
BHEL partnered with E2S Korea for advanced excitation systems tech.
Deal enables design, manufacture, and servicing in India, boosting Make in India.

news.title:
BHEL shares surged 5.4% as it emerged the lowest bidder for Rs 10,300 cr Odisha Genco project.
India's nuclear milestone, Kalpakkam PFBR's criticality, positions BHEL as a key nuclear energy player.
news.summary:
BHEL shares surged 5.4% as it emerged the lowest bidder for Rs 10,300 cr Odisha Genco project.

news.title:
BHEL witnessed a Rs. 24.3 crore block trade on NSE for 984,677 shares.
The trade price per share was Rs. 246.4.

news.title:
BHEL supports India's nuclear program by manufacturing a 700 MWe steam generator.
It develops a 300 MWe reactor with BARC and works on Kudankulam's 2x1000 MWe units.
news.summary:
BHEL supports India's nuclear program by manufacturing a 700 MWe steam generator.

news.title:
India promotes coal gasification, easing foreign tech restrictions while bolstering indigenous efforts by BHEL.
BHEL's Rs 25,000 crore coal-to-ammonium project gets Rs 1,350 crore funding; site development ongoing.
news.summary:
India promotes coal gasification, easing foreign tech restrictions while bolstering indigenous efforts by BHEL.

news.title:
BHEL gets five-year extension to procure 21 items from land border countries.
This ensures extended cross-border sourcing arrangements and supply continuity ahead.
news.summary:
BHEL gets five-year extension to procure 21 items from land border countries.

news.title:
India Ratings revised Bharat Heavy Electricals Ltd's (BHEL) outlook from 'Stable' to 'Positive'.
The long-term rating was reaffirmed at IND AA, with Rs 80,000 Cr loans rated.
news.summary:
India Ratings revised Bharat Heavy Electricals Ltd's (BHEL) outlook from 'Stable' to 'Positive'.

news.title:
Sulphur shortage from West Asia war threatens Bhel's boiler production.
Hormuz blockade impacts Bhel as its boiler order book remains full.
news.summary:

The order reflects fresh demand in BHEL's core power engineering sector.

news.title:
BHEL secures a Rs. 13,500 Cr. NOA from NTPC for 3x800 MW Telangana Stage II STPP.
The project includes design, supply, and commissioning, with completion slated in 62 months.

news.title:

Relaxation follows equipment shortages and delays after 2020 border clash restrictions.

news.title:
Analysts recommend Laurus Labs, BHEL, Granules India, SBI, and Sun Pharma for strong upside, citing technical and fundamental strengths.
Laurus Labs, BHEL, and Sun Pharma show bullish setups; SBI benefits from credit growth, while Granules India leverages cost-efficient production.
